title: Projection ownership and typed events
goal: Restore projection ownership by moving gate/dashboard projection logic out of domain data types and replacing projection-relevant json! event payload coupling with typed event payload constructors/readers.
acceptance: All existing projection snapshots, parity fixtures, and golden output tests remain behavior-preserving unless an intentional divergence is explicitly documented in this slice output.

project_gate_pane and gate/dashboard projection helpers move out of src/domain.rs and into the workflow projection layer or a projection submodule; domain.rs no longer constructs StatusFeed presentation output.

Projection-relevant event payloads used for cockpit readiness, incidents, worker observations, terminal reasons, gate/repair activity, and attention are emitted through typed constructors or typed payload structs rather than ad-hoc json! blobs at call sites.
Projection parses those event payloads through the same typed definitions, with explicit unknown/legacy fallback only where needed for compatibility with old run artifacts.
CLI renderers remain painters over StatusFeed/GatePaneProjection and do not reintroduce raw event-payload interpretation.
The previous 'Cockpit Ready: {raw JSON}' class of leakage is covered by tests that assert human-readable projection text rather than raw payload dumps.
must_ask_if: The implementation would change stored event compatibility so existing run artifacts can no longer be inspected or summarized.
The implementation would move presentation logic from projection into CLI, daemon, state, or domain instead of centralizing it.
The implementation would delete event evidence rather than type the payloads.
The implementation would need to rewrite broad golden fixtures for wording unrelated to projection ownership.
